rows.notes: Clear centered year heading at top of page. No amounts present; very legible.
Right-hand columns align clearly to 1339 5 5. Description transcription uncertain: handwritten word looks like 'Soluta Paria' and abbreviation 'Bro't over' — text legible but orthography ambiguous.
Description 'To W. Davenport - Surveyor' is clear. Numeric columns (39 | 9 | 6) align well with ruling lines and pass validation (s 0-19, d 0-11).
Amount 10 8 0 clearly placed in the three right-hand columns. Name reads as 'Mr Greenshields' (handwriting ligature makes exact spelling slightly uncertain) and 'Do.' (ditto) is clear.

rows.description: the Auditor his deficiency of Salary for the year ending at Michs. 1831
rows.notes: Long description is legible though slightly cramped; there are no amounts written on this line (appears as a sub-item or explanatory entry). Column area for this row is blank.

rows.description: Interest to the Reserve Fund for the Loan of 3068.14.11 at 4% - one year
rows.notes: Amount appears as 122 | 14 | 4½ (pence '4' with a halfpenny mark). Column alignment is clear; fraction interpreted as halfpenny from the small '½' / loop after pence.
Description and full-pound amount 100 0 0 are very clear and well aligned.
Amount 4 | 16 | 11 clearly visible. Description 'Letters, Stamps &c.' easily read though ampersand is handwritten.

rows.description: Plowman for Surveying Barn at Merton
rows.notes: Amount 3 | 14 | 0 is clear. Description partly ambiguous: handwriting looks like 'Plowman for Surveying Barn at Merton' (could be 'Farm' or 'Barn' and 'Merton/Morton'); chosen reading based on letter shapes but some uncertainty remains.

rows.description: To W. Dayman - Donation to Gr. Ind. School
rows.notes: Numeric amount 2 | 0 | 0 is clear. Name and institution abbreviated in handwriting ('W. Dayman' and 'Donation to Gr. Ind School' interpreted from script); there is some uncertainty in abbreviation expansion.

rows.description: Mr Lees. Plowman's Bill for work done in the Channel at Newton
rows.notes: Amount 36 | 14 | 0 well aligned and legible. Description largely readable though a dash and some letters (Lees / Lewis) are slightly stylized; interpreted as 'Mr Lees' with contextual reading of the rest of the line.
Final total at lower right clearly written as '£1659 2 2½' (pence '2' with halfpenny mark). Alignment with ruling lines is clear; fraction interpreted as halfpenny.
